sourceMovie.title: Forrest Gump
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Forrest Gump works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: As Good as It Gets
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Forrest Gump clicked for you, As Good as It Gets (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Forrest Gump?
faq.answer: Try As Good as It Gets, The Apartment, About a Boy—each shares DNA with Forrest Gump while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Forrest Gump?
faq.answer: Forrest Gump (1994) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Forrest Gump worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
